title: Bloody diarrhea in a young adult

chart.oneLiner: 26F presents with several weeks of bloody diarrhea with mucus, urgency, and crampy lower abdominal pain, with recent weight loss.

opening: I keep passing blood and mucus, and I feel like I always have to run to the bathroom.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 26-year-old woman, fatigued, frustrated by symptoms. Good historian.

HPI: 4–6 weeks of frequent BLOODY diarrhea with mucus, fecal URGENCY, TENESMUS, and crampy lower abdominal pain. Up several times at night. ~5 lb weight loss, fatigue. Sometimes joint aches (extraintestinal) and a painful red eye recently (only if asked). NO recent antibiotics, NO travel, NO clear food exposure.
PMH: none. MEDS: none. SOCIAL: nonsmoker; family member with inflammatory bowel disease (if asked).

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: pale, mildly fatigued.
- Vitals: BP 112/70, HR 98, RR 16, 37.6°C, SpO2 99% RA.
- Abdomen: lower abdominal tenderness, no rebound/rigidity (would worsen with toxic megacolon); active bowel sounds.
- Possible aphthous ulcers, joint or eye findings if specifically examined.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the extraintestinal symptoms or family history unless asked about joint/eye/skin symptoms or family history.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.Inflammatory: CRP and ESR elevated; albumin low.
labs.StoolStudies: Stool cultures and C. difficile toxin NEGATIVE (infection excluded).
labs.Endoscopy: Flexible sigmoidoscopy/colonoscopy: continuous, circumferential mucosal inflammation, friability, and ulceration beginning at the rectum — consistent with ulcerative colitis; biopsies pending.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: rSR' in V1 with QRS duration <120 ms (incomplete RBBB).
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: rSR' in V1 with QRS duration <120 ms (incomplete RBBB). Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.
ekg.imageDescription: 12-lead showing incomplete right bundle branch block

cxr.label: Abdominal X-ray (upright)
cxr.findings: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).
cxr.verify: Pick a CC abdominal film that is normal/non-dilated.
cxr.imageDescription: Upright abdominal radiograph, no colonic dilation.

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Ulcerative colitis flare (leading,7). Infectious colitis — must EXCLUDE first, incl. C. difficile (CANT-MISS,5). Crohn's disease (core,3). Ischemic colitis (core,2). Colorectal neoplasm (CANT-MISS to consider,2). Names IBD/UC while prioritizing exclusion of infection (core,6).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: ulcerative colitis flare. Must-not-miss: infectious colitis (must exclude first, incl. C. difficile); colorectal neoplasm. Also reasonable: Crohn's disease, ischemic colitis. Names IBD/UC while prioritizing exclusion of infection.

criticalActions.item: Infectious colitis — must exclude first, incl. C. difficile

coreActions.item: Names IBD/UC while prioritizing exclusion of infection

mustNotMiss: Infectious colitis — must exclude first, incl. C. difficile

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: Stool studies incl. cultures and C. difficile toxin to EXCLUDE infection (CANT-MISS,4). CBC (2). Inflammatory markers — CRP/ESR, albumin (2). Fecal calprotectin (2). Flexible sigmoidoscopy/colonoscopy with biopsy (3). Abdominal imaging if severe (megacolon screen) (2). PENALTY: starting steroids/immunosuppression before excluding infection (-2 — addressed in management).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; CBC; Inflammatory markers; Fecal calprotectin; Stool studies; Endoscopy.

criticalActions.item: Stool studies incl. cultures and C. difficile toxin to exclude infection

penalties.item: Starting steroids/immunosuppression before excluding infection (addressed in management)

requiredTests: Stool studies incl. cultures and C. difficile toxin to exclude infection

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = incomplete right bundle branch block: rSR' in V1 with QRS duration <120 ms. CREDIT: identifies rSR' in V1 (6); recognizes QRS <120 ms — incomplete rather than complete RBBB (5); systematic read (4). PENALTY: calling it complete RBBB without measuring the QRS width, or dismissing it as a normal tracing (-2).
keyFindings: QRS <120 ms — incomplete rather than complete RBBB
commonMistakes: calling it complete RBBB without measuring the QRS width
steps.idealAnswer: Incomplete RBBB: rSR' in V1 with QRS under 120 ms.

coreActions: QRS <120 ms — incomplete rather than complete RBBB

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read X-ray
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's abdominal film. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = no colonic dilation. CREDIT: assesses colonic caliber (5); explicitly screens for/excludes toxic megacolon (5). PENALTY: missing the purpose (-1).
criticalFindings: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).
steps.idealAnswer: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Infection is excluded; endoscopy shows continuous mucosal inflammation from the rectum. What is your diagnosis?
steps.rubric: Ulcerative colitis flare (6). Cites continuous, rectum-up inflammation with negative infectious workup and elevated inflammatory markers/calprotectin (4).
steps.idealAnswer: Ulcerative colitis flare. Supported by continuous, rectum-up mucosal inflammation on endoscopy with a negative infectious workup and elevated inflammatory markers/calprotectin.

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH IBD: IV corticosteroids first-line for severe flare (CANT-MISS,7); rule out infection/C. diff before escalating (CANT-MISS,4); VTE prophylaxis (3); avoid antimotility/opioids/NSAIDs (3); rescue therapy (infliximab/cyclosporine) if steroid-refractory (4); surgery consult for toxic megacolon/perforation/refractory (2); GI consult (2). PENALTY: antimotility/opioids precipitating toxic megacolon (-3); escalating immunosuppression without excluding infection (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Acute severe UC flare: admit; IV corticosteroids (methylprednisolone) first-line. Rule out infection (stool studies, C. diff) and toxic megacolon (abdominal imaging). VTE prophylaxis (high thrombotic risk). Avoid antimotility/opioids/NSAIDs. If steroid-refractory by day 3–5: rescue with infliximab or cyclosporine; surgery consult for refractory disease/perforation/toxic megacolon. GI consult.

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 26F presents with several weeks of bloody diarrhea with mucus, urgency, and crampy lower abdominal pain, with recent weight loss. The diagnosis is Ulcerative colitis flare, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Abdomen: lower abdominal tenderness, no rebound/rigidity (would worsen with toxic megacolon); active bowel sounds.
Abdomen: lower abdominal tenderness, no rebound/rigidity (would worsen with toxic megacolon); active bowel sounds.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Ulcerative colitis flare while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Ulcerative colitis flare and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
criticalWorkupItems: Stool studies incl. cultures and C. difficile toxin to exclude infection
criticalInterpretationItems: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).
keyImageInterpretationFindings: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).
criticalImageInterpretationFindings: No colonic dilation here (used to screen for toxic megacolon in severe flares).
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.

items.item: Shows empathy for the patient's concern
Explains next steps in patient-friendly language
